titleStmt.title: Käthe Oppel (née Eggeling)
born Braunschweig, November 18, 1892; died Tönning, June 25, 1974

publicationStmt.publisher: Schenker Documents Online in association with the Centre for Computing in the Humanities

sourceDesc.p: No source: created in electronic format.

encodingDesc.p: Encoding according to the CCH TEI Guidelines

div.p: The third wife of Reinhard Oppel, mother of Marianne.

Käthe Eggeling took vocal studies and conducting at Kiel Conservatory between 1922 and 1924, during which time she enrolled for courses in score-reading, analysis, counterpoint, and harmony with Reinhard Oppel. She and Oppel were married on December 22, 1926 in St Peter Ording (on the northwest coast, 100km (60 miles) west of Kiel). The couple had one daughter – Reinhard’s sixth child – on November 26, 1927(?): Marianne Elisabeth. The child was born in St Peter Ording, where Käthe had moved after deciding that marriage to Reinhard and life in Kiel were unsuited to her. The couple were divorced on July 17, 1928.

These circumstances perhaps explain why Reinhard made no mention of marriage, birth, or divorce in his correspondence with Heinrich Schenker in the years concerned; all references to his children are to his three sons by his second wife, and those three sons are always looked after either by himself or a maid, and the youngest by a relative or a friend. No references occur to Marianne in Reinhard’s correspondence with Jeanette Schenker in the years 1935 to 1939. Nothing more is known of Käthe.
